key_insight: The paper proposes a human-in-the-loop pipeline that turns complex reasoning algorithms into solvable recurrence equations to automate complexity analysis.
engineering_relevance: It could make cost analysis of symbolic and automated reasoning software faster, more systematic, and less dependent on manual mathematical derivations.
method: User supplies key metrics and helpful lemmas.
A higher-order abstract interpretation step extracts generalized recurrence equations from symbolic programs.
Recurrences are solved with pre/postfixpoint reasoning plus SMT/CAS support to verify bounds.
evidence_signal: The abstract describes a theory and under-development tool and cites example applications, but does not report experimental results or benchmark evaluations.
watch_item: The approach still depends on expert-provided metrics and lemmas, so fully automatic analysis may be limited for difficult or poorly understood algorithms.
paper_title: Towards an Automated Reasoning Tool for Complexity Analysis of Automated Reasoners
